channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: A divided US celebrates 250th birthday as heatwave bears down on East Coast
item.description: The US is celebrating the 250th anniversary of the country's independence on Saturday under sweltering temperatures, with a massive fireworks show planned in Washington following a speech by US President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France to face Paraguay as World Cup last 16 gets under way
item.description: France are bracing for furnace-like temperatures when they face Paraguay in Philadelphia in the World Cup's last 16 on Saturday as co-hosts Canada prepare for a daunting date with African giants Morocco in Houston. With a brutal heatwave roasting swathes of the United States as it celebrates July 4th, Les Bleus take on Paraguay at Lincoln Financial Field against a backdrop of extreme heat warnings.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: New wave of coordinated rebel attacks hits Mali
item.description: Jihadists and their separatist Tuareg allies hit Mali with fresh coordinated attacks Saturday, striking multiple towns and a prison just months after hobbling the country's military junta with a similar wave of assaults. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle explains.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mali government says rebels attacking northern towns
item.description: The Malian army said Saturday that several northern towns, including Gao and Sévaré, were targeted by rebels. The statement came as a rebel group announced a new offensive to capture a northern town. Mohamed Elmaouloud Ramadane, a spokesperson for ​the Azawad Liberation Front (FLA), said in a Facebook post that the town of Anefis was being targeted by the separatists. FRANCE 24's Wassim Nasr reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran holds funeral for Ali Khamenei: Attendees 'hardline factions' of society, specialist says
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran on Saturday for the start of a dayslong funeral for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Tara Kangarlou, a global affairs journalist and author of The Heartbeat of Iran, said most attendees were "more emboldened, hardline factions of the society," making the crowd unrepresentative of broader public attitudes toward the regime.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pop star Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married Friday night at Madison Square Garden, where actor Adam Sandler was the surprising officiant at a ceremony and Stevie Nicks performed among a crowd packed with stars of sports and entertainment.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Tour de France kicks off in Barcelona
item.description: The annual men's cycling race, Tour de France, kicked off on Saturday in Barcelona amid a festive atmosphere.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026 : Cape Verde miss out on greatest upset
item.description: Newcomers Cape Verde almost created the upset of the century by forcing the reigning champions Argentina into extra time, unfortunately the blue sharks conceded in extra time. Argentina will therefore face Egypt in the round of 16. There was not much in it between Australia's Socceroos' and the Egyptian Pharoes, the two teams went all the way to penalties, where Egypt proved extremely efficient, scoring every single shot from the penalty spot. An early Colombian goal meanwhile proved insurmountable for Ghana, the black stars will head home without any shot on target in that knockout game. France gears up for their game against Panama, and Canada face Morocco in the first round of 16 fixture.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: New Zealand's Rennie expresses 'relief' as All Blacks edge narrow win over France
item.description: The All Blacks began their 2026 Nations Championship tenure with a narrow win over France on Saturday, with freshly appointed coach Dave Rennie promising the team was only going to get better as they head into future matches.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Zelensky denies Russian capture of key eastern city
item.description: Ukraine still controls the strategically important eastern city of Kostiantynivka, President ​Volodymyr Zelensky and the General Staff said on Saturday, rejecting Russian claims that it has been captured. Russia's military told President ​Vladimir Putin ‌on Friday that its forces had taken ⁠control of Kostiantynivka, a target that Moscow has long sought in its advance ‌through the Donetsk region.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump speech at Mount Rushmore: Making it clear 'who's in and who's out', expert says
item.description: President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of American independence on Friday with soaring rhetoric about American exceptionalism before veering into a darkly political speech with warnings about a sinister threat of communism that evoked one of the country’s ugliest chapters. Ian Reifowitz, SUNY Distinguished Professor of history at SUNY-Empire State University, said Trump has "one goal - make himself more popular and more powerful," as he pointed to how Trump has politicised the celebrations through his speech.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Argentina see off spirited Cape Verde to qualify for World Cup last 16
item.description: The reigning World Cup champions were pushed all the way by an impressive Cape Verde team in their Round of 32 clash, needing an own goal in extra-time to emerge 3-2 victors. Lionel Messi scored the 20th World Cup goal of his career.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Firefighters combat blazes in northern Spain, Portugal
item.description: Hundreds of Spanish firefighters battled an expanding wildfire near the tourist hotspot of Costa Brava on Friday as Portugal sought outside help to combat forest fires in its north.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mali insurgents attack multiple towns and a prison, army says
item.description: Insurgents in ​Mali staged attacks in at least five locations ‌early ⁠on ‌Saturday, including at a prison in Kenieroba, according to an army statement. The fighting comes more than two months after the country's defence minister was killed.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 15 million people expected to attend former supreme leader's funeral
item.description: Mourners thronged a vast prayer complex in ​Tehran on Saturday as the week-long funeral ceremonies of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began with the national anthem, religious eulogies and readings from the Koran. 15 million people are expected to attend the former leader's funeral, according to Iranian authorities, FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah said, reporting from Tehran, adding that the event would take place also in neighbouring Iraq.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pope Leo's visits to Lampedusa on July 4 'symbolic'
item.description: Pope Leo XIV, who has sparred with the Trump administration over its immigration crackdown, spent the Fourth of July on Saturday in the epicenter of Europe’s migration debate to honor the tens of thousands of people who have died trying to reach Europe to find freedom and prosperity. While the United States marks the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with rallies, parties and fireworks, history’s first US-born pope traveled to the Sicilian island of Lampedusa to pray at a migrant cemetery and celebrate a solemn Mass for the island's newest arrivals. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Foreign rescue workers help with recovering Venezuela's quake victims
item.description: With little hope of finding loved ones alive, frustrated families of those affected by Venezuela's double earthquakes clamored for help to recover their loved one's bodies Friday as uncertainty grew over the death toll from the tragedy. Authorities on Friday reported a total of 2,645 deaths and more than 12,000 wounded from the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes that struck the country last week, with most fatalities in the coastal town of La Guaira, where scores of residential complexes were flattened.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump marks US 250th celebration with speech and Mount Rushmore flyover
item.description: US President Donald ​Trump called on Americans to protect the freedoms the nation's founders envisioned 250 years ago against what he has portrayed as the "communist" threat posed by progressive Democrats, speaking on the eve of Independence Day at Mount Rushmore.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: England face Mexico in Azteca fortress, returning to scene of Maradona handball
item.description: Thomas Tuchel’s stuttering England are an ideal target for co-hosts Mexico as they seek a prestigious World Cup win in their final game at the near-impregnable Azteca stadium, the scene of the infamous Maradona handball that still haunts the Three Lions decades later.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: 250 years of US independence: How France helped turn the tide of the Revolutionary War
item.description: After months of discreet support, France officially allied itself with the American revolutionaries in their war against the British Empire, a decision that would permanently alter the fate of both nations.
item.dc:creator: Barbara GABEL

item.title: US 'royal wedding': Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce marry at Madison Square Garden
item.description: US pop queen Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce officially tied the knot on Friday, triggering frenzied global attention around America's "royal wedding". The couple made no public announcement about their wedding plans and reportedly insisted that guests sign non-disclosure agreements.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Pope defends migrants on symbolic visit to key immigration hub Lampedusa
item.description: Pope Leo XIV arrived on Saturday on Italy's Lampedusa island, a major port of call for migrants who risked the perilous crossing from North Africa, in a symbolic rebuke to the anti-immigrant rhetoric coming from US and EU political factions.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump hails American exceptionalism, evokes 'communist menace' at 250th celebration
item.description: US President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of US independence on Friday with rhetoric about American exceptionalism before calling on the country to fight what he called the sinister threat of communism. The speech evoked the Red Scare of the 1950s, one of the country’s ugliest chapters, when alleged communists were persecuted and blacklisted from jobs across America.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Thousands flock to Iran's funeral rites for late supreme leader Khamenei
item.description: Thousands of mourners flocked to a vast prayer complex in Tehran on Saturday as the week-long funeral ceremonies for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began. Authorities shut down streets, airspace and daily life in Tehran to commemorate the life of the man who for decades ruled with an iron fist while confronting the West. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
